title: Hook
description: Method `hook` (host → ext, request). An intercept: the host awaits the extension's `HookOutcome` and folds it into a chain across extensions in load order (each sees the prior patch). On timeout or crash the host applies the hook's default — fail-closed (block) for `tool_call`/`user_bash`, fail-open (continue) for the rest. Timeouts are manifest-overridable via `hook_timeout_ms`.

input.description: The value being intercepted (e.g. `{ tool, arguments }` for `tool_call`). Shape is hook-specific; extensions read what they understand and ignore the rest.

HookOutcome.title: HookOutcome
HookOutcome.description: An extension's reply to a `hook`. `continue` proceeds unchanged; `block` vetoes (honored for every hook, and the default for fail-closed hooks on timeout); `modify` replaces the intercepted value with `patch`, which the next extension in the chain then sees.

reason.description: Human-readable veto reason, surfaced to the model/user.
